reasoning: Without the ability to pull real-time sentiment or specific upcoming earnings/dividend catalysts for Prologis (PLD) just prior to the cutoff date, I must rely on base rates. Historically, stocks and REITs exhibit a slight positive drift over time, meaning the probability that the price is higher than a given baseline typically starts slightly above 50% due to the equity risk premium. However, individual stocks have substantial idiosyncratic volatility. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I temper the expected positive drift as the horizon extends. I start with 53% for the one-week horizon, reducing to 52% for the one-month horizon, and 51% for the three-month horizon into February 2026, capturing the high level of uncertainty for individual stock returns over these periods.
